Class GameViewHex

java.lang.Object
fr.uge.cascadia.view.GameViewHex
public class GameViewHex extends Object
Manages the graphical rendering and user interactions for the Cascadia game with hexagonal tiles.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    GameViewHex(GameManager gameManager, ImageLoader imageLoader)
    Creates a new instance of GameViewHex.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    animalPlacementError(Graphics2D graphics, Animal animal)
    Displays an error message when an animal cannot be placed on the board.
    Animal
    chooseAnimalClick(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext context, int startX, int startY, int animalSize, int verticalSpacing, FauneBag animals)
    Handles the player's interaction to select an animal by clicking.
    TileAnimalPair
    chooseHexLotClick(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext context, int startX, int startY, int tileSize, int verticalSpacing, Tiles tiles, FauneBag animals)
    Handles the player's interaction to select a hexagonal tile-animal pair by clicking.
    Tile
    chooseTileClick(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext context, int startX, int startY, int tileSize, int verticalSpacing, Tiles tiles)
    Handles the player's interaction to select a tile by clicking.
    TileAnimalPair
    finalLotChoice(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext context, int startX, int startY, int tileSize, int verticalSpacing, String name, Tiles tiles, FauneBag animals)
    Handles the final choice of a lot (tile-animal pair) by a player, considering the use of nature tokens.
    double
    getRotation(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext context, TileAnimalPair selectedPair)
    Manages the rotation of a selected tile within a TileAnimalPair.
    int
    placeAnimalClick(com.github.forax.zen.Event event, Animal animal, String name, int startX, int startY, int tileSize)
    Handles the placement of an animal on the board by a player via mouse click.
    int
    placeHexTileClick(com.github.forax.zen.Event event, Tile tile, String name, int startX, int startY, int tileSize)
    Handles the placement of a hexagonal tile by a player via mouse click.
    void
    render(Graphics2D graphics, String name)
    Renders the player's game interface, including their board and available actions.
    void
    renderLot(Graphics2D graphics, TileAnimalPair selectedPair)
    Renders the selected tile-animal pair in the interface.
    static void

    Rotates an image by a specified angle and draws it on the graphics context.
    void

    Displays the combined score of a player in Zen mode, including animal, tile, and nature token scores.
    void
    showHexLot(Graphics2D graphics, int startX, int startY)
    Displays the current available lots of hexagonal tiles and animals on the game screen.
    void
    showSelectedLot(Graphics2D graphics, TileAnimalPair selectedPair, int startX, int startY, int tileSize)
    Displays the selected tile-animal pair on the game screen.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameViewHex

      public GameViewHex(GameManager gameManager, ImageLoader imageLoader)
      Creates a new instance of GameViewHex.
      Parameters:
      gameManager - the game manager to manage game state and logic
      imageLoader - the image loader for fetching graphical assets
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if gameManager or imageLoader is null

  • Method Details

    • render

      public void render(Graphics2D graphics, String name)
      Renders the player's game interface, including their board and available actions.
      Parameters:
      graphics - the graphics context used for rendering
      name - the name of the player whose interface is being rendered
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if graphics or name is null

    • renderLot

      public void renderLot(Graphics2D graphics, TileAnimalPair selectedPair)
      Renders the selected tile-animal pair in the interface.
      Parameters:
      graphics - the graphics context used for rendering
      selectedPair - the tile-animal pair selected by the player
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if graphics or selectedPair is null

    • rotateImage

      public static void rotateImage(BufferedImage image, double angle, Graphics2D graphics, int centerX, int centerY)
      Rotates an image by a specified angle and draws it on the graphics context.
      Parameters:
      image - the image to rotate
      angle - the rotation angle in radians
      graphics - the graphics context used for rendering
      centerX - the X-coordinate for the center of rotation
      centerY - the Y-coordinate for the center of rotation
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if graphics or image is null

    • showHexLot

      public void showHexLot(Graphics2D graphics, int startX, int startY)
      Displays the current available lots of hexagonal tiles and animals on the game screen.
      Parameters:
      graphics - the graphics context used for rendering
      startX - the X-coordinate of the starting position for displaying lots
      startY - the Y-coordinate of the starting position for displaying lots
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if graphics is null

    • showSelectedLot

      public void showSelectedLot(Graphics2D graphics, TileAnimalPair selectedPair, int startX, int startY, int tileSize)
      Displays the selected tile-animal pair on the game screen.
      Parameters:
      graphics - the graphics context used for rendering
      selectedPair - the selected tile-animal pair
      startX - the X-coordinate of the starting position
      startY - the Y-coordinate of the starting position
      tileSize - the size of the hexagonal tile
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if graphics or selectedPair is null

    • chooseHexLotClick

      public TileAnimalPair chooseHexLotClick(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext context, int startX, int startY, int tileSize, int verticalSpacing, Tiles tiles, FauneBag animals)
      Handles the player's interaction to select a hexagonal tile-animal pair by clicking.
      Parameters:
      context - the application context for handling user events
      startX - the X-coordinate of the starting position for displaying lots
      startY - the Y-coordinate of the starting position for displaying lots
      tileSize - the size of the hexagonal tiles
      verticalSpacing - the vertical spacing between displayed lots
      tiles - the Tiles object managing game tiles
      animals - the FauneBag containing the available animals
      Returns:
      the selected TileAnimalPair
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if context, tiles, or animals is null

    • chooseTileClick

      public Tile chooseTileClick(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext context, int startX, int startY, int tileSize, int verticalSpacing, Tiles tiles)
      Handles the player's interaction to select a tile by clicking.
      Parameters:
      context - the application context for handling user events
      startX - the X-coordinate of the starting position for displaying tiles
      startY - the Y-coordinate of the starting position for displaying tiles
      tileSize - the size of the hexagonal tiles
      verticalSpacing - the vertical spacing between displayed tiles
      tiles - the Tiles object managing game tiles
      Returns:
      the selected Tile
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if context or tiles is null

    • chooseAnimalClick

      public Animal chooseAnimalClick(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext context, int startX, int startY, int animalSize, int verticalSpacing, FauneBag animals)
      Handles the player's interaction to select an animal by clicking.
      Parameters:
      context - the application context for handling user events
      startX - the X-coordinate of the starting position for displaying animals
      startY - the Y-coordinate of the starting position for displaying animals
      animalSize - the size of the animal images
      verticalSpacing - the vertical spacing between displayed animals
      animals - the FauneBag containing the available animals
      Returns:
      the selected Animal
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if context or animals is null

    • finalLotChoice

      public TileAnimalPair finalLotChoice(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext context, int startX, int startY, int tileSize, int verticalSpacing, String name, Tiles tiles, FauneBag animals)
      Handles the final choice of a lot (tile-animal pair) by a player, considering the use of nature tokens.
      Parameters:
      context - the application context for handling user events
      startX - the X-coordinate for displaying lots
      startY - the Y-coordinate for displaying lots
      tileSize - the size of the hexagonal tiles
      verticalSpacing - the vertical spacing between displayed lots
      name - the name of the player making the choice
      tiles - the Tiles object managing available tiles
      animals - the FauneBag containing the available animals
      Returns:
      the selected TileAnimalPair
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if context, name, tiles, or animals is null

    • getRotation

      public double getRotation(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext context, TileAnimalPair selectedPair)
      Manages the rotation of a selected tile within a TileAnimalPair.
      Parameters:
      context - the application context for handling user events
      selectedPair - the selected tile-animal pair
      Returns:
      the final rotation angle of the tile
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if context or selectedPair is null

    • placeHexTileClick

      public int placeHexTileClick(com.github.forax.zen.Event event, Tile tile, String name, int startX, int startY, int tileSize)
      Handles the placement of a hexagonal tile by a player via mouse click.
      Parameters:
      event - the event representing a mouse click
      tile - the tile to be placed
      name - the name of the player placing the tile
      startX - the X-coordinate for the board's starting position
      startY - the Y-coordinate for the board's starting position
      tileSize - the size of the hexagonal tiles
      Returns:
      1 if the tile was successfully placed, otherwise 0
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if tile or name is null

    • placeAnimalClick

      public int placeAnimalClick(com.github.forax.zen.Event event, Animal animal, String name, int startX, int startY, int tileSize)
      Handles the placement of an animal on the board by a player via mouse click.
      Parameters:
      event - the event representing a mouse click
      animal - the animal to be placed
      name - the name of the player placing the animal
      startX - the X-coordinate for the board's starting position
      startY - the Y-coordinate for the board's starting position
      tileSize - the size of the hexagonal tiles
      Returns:
      1 if the animal was successfully placed, otherwise 0
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if animal or name is null

    • animalPlacementError

      public void animalPlacementError(Graphics2D graphics, Animal animal)
      Displays an error message when an animal cannot be placed on the board.
      Parameters:
      graphics - the graphics context used for rendering
      animal - the animal that cannot be placed
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if graphics or animal is null

    • showCombinedScoreZen

      public void showCombinedScoreZen(Graphics2D graphics, Map<Animal,Integer> animalScores, Map<Habitat,Integer> tileScores, Map<Habitat,Integer> bonuses, int natureToken, String name)
      Displays the combined score of a player in Zen mode, including animal, tile, and nature token scores.
      Parameters:
      graphics - the graphics context used for rendering
      animalScores - a map containing scores for each animal type
      tileScores - a map containing scores for each habitat type
      bonuses - a map containing bonus scores for habitats
      natureToken - the number of nature tokens the player has
      name - the name of the player
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any parameter is null

Generated by Ant
